payload.title: Reconcile README with what ships + add an 'advisory linter' quickstart
payload.description: Reviewer: 'the install command and [package.metadata.trust] section describe unshipped tooling; that sent me down the wrong path initially.' Reality check: cargo-trustc, trust-rustc, trust-rustdoc, trust-lang ARE published at 0.1.0 — but the reviewer evidently didn't get the wrapper (old install line listed only trust-lang). Fix: (a) verify `cargo install trust-lang cargo-trustc trust-rustc trust-rustdoc` from crates.io yields a working `cargo trustc build` on a clean machine, and make the install path foolproof; (b) add a top-of-README 'advisory linter, no dialect' quickstart for the exact use the reviewer wanted (run the safety lints out-of-tree); (c) be explicit that #![strict] is NOT valid stock Rust and only works through the wrapper, pointing committed-source users at the metadata activation.
